cooked:
Hi there. I was wondering if anyone has got a list of all the entries of anims.hqr
I remember starting doing that myself, but I stopped at entry 152 because animations were starting to get hard to figure out by swapping them with Twinsens ones.
(by the way, I found a few interesting unused animations such as Twinsen flying on a malfuncioning proto-pack and blowing on Gawleys horn)
raw:
Hi there. I was wondering if anyone has got a list of all the entries of anims.hqr
I remember starting doing that myself, but I stopped at entry 152 because animations were starting to get hard to "figure out" by swapping them with Twinsen's ones.
(by the way, I found a few interesting unused animations such as Twinsen flying on a malfuncioning proto-pack and blowing on Gawley's horn)

cooked: - Lawrence: Look at the LBA File Information site. The list for the LBA 1 ANIM.HQR file is complete, the LBA 2 one is in-complete so far.
raw: \- Lawrence: Look at the LBA File Information site. The list for the LBA 1 ANIM.HQR file is complete, the LBA 2 one is in-complete so far. ;)

cooked: Blowing on gawleys horn is used isnt it? Maybe the malfunctioning proto-pack is where you fall from a cliff or something while trying to float.
raw: Blowing on gawleys horn is used isn't it? Maybe the "malfunctioning proto-pack" is where you fall from a cliff or something while trying to float.

cooked:
Big whoops! I didnt mention that Im talking about LBA2 here. Stupid me.
This is why I was surprised of seeing Gawleys horn animation in there, and the protopack animation is not the one from falling off a cliff, Twinsen actually flies but in a wobbly manner, sometimes quickly spinning 360.
You can check those out yourself if you want, the entries of anims.hqr are:
#117 Gawleys horn
#122 Protopack malfunction

So the list is still uncomplete huh? Ah well. It seems nothing has changed since the last time I checked. I could post all the entries I found out if someone wants/needs them.
raw:
Big whoops! I didn't mention that I'm talking about LBA2 here. Stupid me. :P
This is why I was surprised of seeing Gawley's horn animation in there, and the protopack animation is not the one from falling off a cliff, Twinsen actually flies but in a wobbly manner, sometimes quickly spinning 360. 
You can check those out yourself if you want, the entries of anims.hqr are:
#117 Gawley's horn
#122 Protopack malfunction
 
So the list is still uncomplete huh? Ah well. It seems nothing has changed since the last time I checked. I could post all the entries I found out if someone wants/needs them.

cooked: - Lawrence: Sure, it would be a great contribution, if you posted the entries you found out.
raw: \- Lawrence: Sure, it would be a great contribution, if you posted the entries you found out. ;)

cooked: Thanks for the info, I took the liberty of adding it into the file info site, I hope you dont have anything against that :).

cooked: I believe Twinsen does a 360 turn wit the proto-pack in LBA2 if he is hit by something (loss of life)
raw: I believe Twinsen does a 360 turn wit the proto-pack in LBA2 if he is hit by something (loss of life)...

cooked: Nah, 100% sure its not that one. You have to see the animation in action to understand what I mean. He doesnt simply turn 360, he actually flies forward in an unstable manner, sometimes spinning. Try switching that animation with Twinsens standing animation or something and youll see.
raw: Nah, 100% sure it's not that one. You have to see the animation in action to understand what I mean. He doesn't simply turn 360, he actually flies forward in an unstable manner, sometimes spinning. Try switching that animation with Twinsen's standing animation or something and you'll see.

cooked: Sorry, I would, but I dont really know how to make a video of a game, nor I have the necessary program to do that Anyway its not really long to do by yourself, you just need LBAPackEditor, swap the malfunctioning protopack entry with twinsens standing animation entry, boot LBA2 and watch.
raw: Sorry, I would, but I don't really know how to make a video of a game, nor I have the necessary program to do that... Anyway it's not really long to do by yourself, you just need LBAPackEditor, swap the "malfunctioning protopack" entry with twinsen's standing animation entry, boot LBA2 and watch.
